violations.text: The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment is not to use dishwasher until it is able to reach the proper chlorine sanitizing solution. PIC called for service. 3-bay sink was set-up at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils can opener were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was moved to 3-bay sink at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen and cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026
Various food was observed stored on the floor in the temporary walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer were observed non-operational and in poor repair. Equipment must be in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the dishwasher have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The floor and wall junctures to the left of the walk-in cooler do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026

The floor in the kitchen area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment is to sanitize in the 3-bay sink until dishwasher is able to reach the proper chlorine sanitizing solution. 3-bay sink was set-up at the time of inspection. Establishment is to send proof of repair to inspector. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025

The door to the outside, located in the lunch room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the dry storage area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025
The walk-in cooler and walk-in freezer gaskets were observed in poor repair. Per PIC, gaskets are on order. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025

The floor in the walk-in cooler area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The floor in the dry storage area has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment to use 3 bay sink for sanitizing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

Various chemicals were stored on the same shelf as utensils. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024
Walk-in cooler door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 10 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. The 3 bay sink will be used to sanitize equipment and utensils until the dishwasher is sanitizing properly. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and slicer,) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener and slicer are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
Food observed stored on shelves in the dry storage area that are not at least 6 inches above the floor. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Utensils for dry ingredients observed on unclean surfaces (top of containers). Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean on shelves in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of kitchen shelves, can opener and can opener base, dishwasher, and refrigerator have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

The fan cover in the walk in cooler has an accumulation of dust and debris. The walk in floor has an accumulation of food debris. Kitchen doors have an accumulation of soil residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
